255.reason: The condition `line.charAt(p) != ' '` is too restrictive; it should also check for tab characters and other whitespace, but more critically, the condition `!reservedChars.contains(String.valueOf(line.charAt(p)))` incorrectly treats reserved characters like '{', '}', ';', etc. as part of an identifier, causing them to be absorbed into the token content instead of being recognized as separate tokens. This leads to tokens like "printf" being parsed as an identifier with extra whitespace or characters, and keywords like "return" being absorbed into an identifier token.

238.reason: IndexOutOfBoundsException risk: p is incremented before checking bounds, and if p equals line.length(), charAt(p) will throw an exception. Also, logic for handling end of string inside loop is flawed as it accesses charAt(p) after incrementing p without checking if p < line.length().
